 {"id":"beads_rust-x7on","title":"Artifact report indexer (HTML/Markdown summaries)","description":"Generate human-friendly reports from test artifacts for faster triage.

Scope
- Summarize per-suite results, durations, failures, and artifact paths.
- Generate HTML/Markdown report with links to stdout/stderr/snapshots.
- Optional: embed JSON diffs for conformance failures.

Acceptance
- Report generation is optional but easy to run and included in docs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Opus-C","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:53:18.994702303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:43:31.447388278Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:43:31.447388278Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: HTML/Markdown report generation implemented and tested (118 tests passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7on","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7on","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-no03","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7on","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-r23m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7on","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Epic coordinator review (Opus-45-Claude):

The implementation appears complete:
- tests/common/report_indexer.rs: Full ArtifactIndexer implementation with HTML/Markdown generation
- tests/e2e_report_generation.rs: Comprehensive E2E tests (all 6 tests passing)
- scripts/generate-report.sh: CLI script for generating reports

All acceptance criteria met:
✓ Summarizes per-suite results, durations, failures, artifact paths
✓ Generates HTML/Markdown report with links to stdout/stderr/snapshots
✓ Report generation is optional and easy to run
✓ Included in docs (scripts/generate-report.sh has usage instructions)

Recommend closing this task. Tests verified passing:
cargo test --test e2e_report_generation: 118 passed, 1 ignored (manual test)","created_at":"2026-01-18T07:31:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-x7z8","title":"Dataset registry + safe copy from real repos","description":"Create a dataset registry that uses real .beads directories as fixtures and copies them into isolated temp workspaces (no mocks, no mutation of sources).

Scope
- Register datasets: /data/projects/beads_viewer, /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search, /data/projects/brenner_bot, /data/projects/beads_rust.
- Copy .beads (and minimal repo scaffold) into temp workspace for each test run.
- Capture dataset metadata (issue count, jsonl/db sizes, dependency counts) for logging/benchmark baselines.

Acceptance
- Fixture copy is read-only to the source path and writes only to temp/target/test-datasets.
- Dataset metadata is emitted in test logs and summary.json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:35.307131500Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:05:13.926239469Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:05:13.926239469Z","close_reason":"Implemented dataset registry with safe copy mechanism","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7z8","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7z8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes: dataset copy should respect sync allowlist (ignore .git and non-.beads files). If a dataset has merge artifacts (beads.left.jsonl, etc.), ensure copy preserves them for conformance tests but never treats them as primary JSONL unless explicitly requested.","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-x8ze","title":"Fuzz merge_issue three-way conflict invariants","description":"Testing-fuzzing follow-up. Add a coverage-guided or property fuzz harness for the merge_issue three-way logic.

Scope:
- Generate randomized base/local/external issue triples, including tombstones, closed/open status changes, priority/type/assignee edits, label and dependency changes, timestamp skew, empty fields, and conflicting content hashes.
- Feed triples into the same merge_issue path used by sync --merge.
- Assert termination, no panic, deterministic output for identical inputs, and structured conflict reporting for unresolved conflicts.
- Assert invariants: chosen issue has a valid ID/status/type/priority, timestamps remain parseable, tombstones do not resurrect accidentally, force-db/force-jsonl/force modes choose the documented side, and no duplicate labels/dependencies are emitted.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness can run in a bounded CI mode and a longer local fuzz mode.
- Corpus contains hand-written seeds for delete-vs-update, close-vs-edit, equal timestamp conflicts, prefix mismatch, and dependency/tombstone interactions.
- Any minimized crash or invariant failure can be promoted to a normal regression test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:16.244834730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:16.244834730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"beads_rust-xclc","title":"Fuzz content-hash stability across formatting variants","description":"Testing-fuzzing follow-up. Add a property/fuzz harness for issue content-hash computation.

Scope:
- Generate randomized Issue-like inputs and semantically equivalent formatting variants.
- Vary whitespace, field ordering where parsed from JSON, line endings, optional empty-vs-missing fields when the model treats them equivalently, label ordering if normalized, and description/design/acceptance formatting that should not affect the canonical hash.
- Assert hash determinism across repeated runs and documented formatting-insensitive variants.
- Assert hash changes when semantically meaningful fields change.
- Capture any current non-invariant behavior as explicit documented exclusions rather than silent assumptions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness or proptest target covers canonical content-hash generation.
- Properties distinguish formatting-insensitive normalization from meaningful content changes.
- Seeds include whitespace-only edits, CRLF/LF pairs, reordered labels where applicable, empty optional fields, and large unicode text.
- Failures shrink to a reproducible seed/test case.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:08.536099902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:08.536099902Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-xclc","title":"Fuzz content-hash stability across formatting variants","description":"Testing-fuzzing follow-up. Add a property/fuzz harness for issue content-hash computation.

Scope:
- Generate randomized Issue-like inputs and semantically equivalent formatting variants.
- Vary whitespace, field ordering where parsed from JSON, line endings, optional empty-vs-missing fields when the model treats them equivalently, label ordering if normalized, and description/design/acceptance formatting that should not affect the canonical hash.
- Assert hash determinism across repeated runs and documented formatting-insensitive variants.
- Assert hash changes when semantically meaningful fields change.
- Capture any current non-invariant behavior as explicit documented exclusions rather than silent assumptions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness or proptest target covers canonical content-hash generation.
- Properties distinguish formatting-insensitive normalization from meaningful content changes.
- Seeds include whitespace-only edits, CRLF/LF pairs, reordered labels where applicable, empty optional fields, and large unicode text.
- Failures shrink to a reproducible seed/test case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyDune","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:08.536099902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:32:37.451527776Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:32:37.451272959Z","close_reason":"Added content-hash cargo-fuzz harness for formatting-stability invariants and documented meaningful text-format exclusions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":294,"issue_id":"beads_rust-xclc","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented content-hash cargo-fuzz target covering deterministic hashing, compact/pretty/randomized JSON formatting, field order, CRLF/LF parsing, unknown JSON fields, empty optional equivalence, NUL-to-space canonicalization, ignored metadata/relations, and meaningful content changes. Documented that whitespace inside included issue text remains meaningful content. Verified with cargo fuzz check/run, cargo check, clippy, rustfmt, and ubs; manifest-wide fuzz fmt is currently blocked by a concurrent merge_issue bin entry without its source file.","created_at":"2026-04-21T22:32:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-xewv","title":"Conformance: Advanced Commands (epic, query, graph, changelog, history, audit)","description":"# Conformance: Advanced Commands

## Purpose
Verify br vs bd JSON parity for advanced commands: epic, query, graph, changelog, history, audit.

## Current State
NONE of these commands have conformance tests yet.

## Test Specifications

### epic Command (8 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_epic_status | Show epic with children | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_epic_add_child | Add issue to epic | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_epic_remove_child | Remove from epic | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_epic_close_eligible_true | All children closed | ExactJson |
| conformance_epic_close_eligible_false | Open children exist | ExactJson |
| conformance_epic_nested | Epic containing epic | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_epic_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_epic_not_epic_error | Non-epic issue | ExitCodeOnly |

### query Command (7 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_query_basic | Simple status filter | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_query_type_filter | type:bug | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_query_priority_filter | priority:<=1 | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_query_boolean_and | status:open AND type:bug | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_query_boolean_or | type:bug OR type:feature | ArrayUnordered |
| conformance_query_json_shape | JSON output | StructureOnly |
| conformance_query_syntax_error | Invalid query | ExitCodeOnly |

### graph Command (6 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_graph_empty | No dependencies | ExactJson |
| conformance_graph_simple | A→B | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_graph_complex | Multiple connections | StructureOnly |
| conformance_graph_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_graph_dot_format | --format dot | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_graph_filter_type | --type epic | NormalizedJson |

### changelog Command (6 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_changelog_basic | Closed issues listed | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_changelog_date_filter | --since flag | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_changelog_type_filter | --type feature | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_changelog_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_changelog_empty | No closed issues | ExactJson |
| conformance_changelog_markdown | --format md | ExitCodeOnly |

### history Command (5 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_history_list | List backups | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_history_save | Create backup | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_history_restore | Restore backup | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_history_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_history_empty | No backups | ExactJson |

### audit Command (5 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_audit_basic | Audit log output | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_audit_filter_issue | --issue filter | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_audit_filter_action | --action filter | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_audit_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_audit_empty | No interactions | ExactJson |

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`rust
// Standard logging for each test
fn log_conformance_result(
    test_name: &str,
    br_args: &[&str],
    bd_args: &[&str],
    br_out: &CmdOutput,
    bd_out: &CmdOutput,
    compare_mode: &CompareMode,
    result: &Result<(), String>
) {
    info!("=== {} ===", test_name);
    info!("  br_cmd: {:?}", br_args);
    info!("  bd_cmd: {:?}", bd_args);
    info!("  br_exit: {}", br_out.status);
    info!("  bd_exit: {}", bd_out.status);
    info!("  br_duration: {:?}", br_out.duration);
    info!("  bd_duration: {:?}", bd_out.duration);
    info!("  compare_mode: {:?}", compare_mode);
    match result {
        Ok(()) => info!("  result: PASS"),
        Err(e) => {
            error!("  result: FAIL");
            error!("  diff: {}", e);
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 37 new conformance tests
- [ ] All commands have JSON parity with bd
- [ ] Structured logging for every test
- [ ] Test failures include detailed diff output","notes":"SCOPE UPDATE: Only 3 of 6 commands have bd equivalents for conformance testing:
- epic: bd has this (8 tests possible)
- graph: bd has this (6 tests possible)
- audit: bd has this (5 tests possible)

Commands WITHOUT bd parity (br-only features, NO conformance tests):
- query: br only (bd doesn't have query command)
- changelog: br only (bd doesn't have changelog command)
- history: br only (bd doesn't have history command)

Total: 19 conformance tests instead of 37.

Work started by OpusAgent on 2026-01-17.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusAgent","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:10:48.577183305Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:24:49.333512177Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:24:49.333512177Z","close_reason":"Implemented 19 conformance tests for epic (8), graph (6), and audit (5) commands. 1 epic test ignored - documents known br/bd difference in parent-child dependency blocking semantics. query/changelog/history commands skipped - no bd parity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-xewv","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-egz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ cargo-fuzz = true
 [dependencies]
 beads_rust = { path = "..", default-features = false }
 libfuzzer-sys = "0.4"
+serde_json = "1.0.149"
 tempfile = "3.25"
 
 [[bin]]
@@ -18,3 +19,10 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/jsonl_import.rs"
 test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "content_hash"
+path = "fuzz_targets/content_hash.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false

@@ -8,11 +8,24 @@ Run the JSONL import harness in bounded mode:
 cargo fuzz run jsonl_import -- -runs=10000 -max_len=65536
 ```
 
+Run the content-hash harness in bounded mode:
+
+```bash
+cargo fuzz run content_hash -- -runs=10000 -max_len=4096
+```
+
 Run a compile check without fuzzing:
 
 ```bash
 cargo fuzz check jsonl_import
+cargo fuzz check content_hash
 ```
 
 The harnesses create isolated temporary workspaces and must not read or write
 the repository's real `.beads/` directory.
+
+The `content_hash` target treats JSON serialization whitespace, JSON field
+order, unknown JSON fields, empty optional fields, labels, relationships, and
+metadata-only issue fields as formatting-insensitive. Whitespace inside included
+issue text fields such as title, description, design, acceptance criteria, and
+notes is intentionally treated as meaningful issue content.
